discussion_comments.comment_body: We shouldn't show this if user is logged in.
Looks like duplicate code. Also, it could be improved.
yeah, i'll look into it tmrw. im having this issue where i wasnt able to login or signup as a user to check these limitations once ran the project locally idk why, could you help me with it pls??

I meant that above you have such code: 

` const validKey = USERNAME_REGEX.test(keyCode) && username.length <= 20;`

and here the same statement. 

I have two solutions, how to improve code:

### I. Improve USERNAME_REGEX

You don't need always check `USERNAME_REGEX.test(value) && value.length <= 20;` 
In 60th line you can make regex like this:

```diff
- const USERNAME_REGEX = /^[a-zA-Z0-9]*$/;
+ const USERNAME_REGEX = /^[a-zA-Z0-9]{0,20}$/;
```

then only use `.test(value)`
```ts
const isValid = USERNAME_REGEX.test(value);
```

here is an example:
https://regex101.com/r/XVbgdc/1


### II. Create function to check

```diff
const USERNAME_REGEX = /^[a-zA-Z0-9]*$/;
+const isUserNameValid= (value: string) =>  USERNAME_REGEX.test(value) && value.length <= 20;
```

and you don't need to create constants like this:

```ts
const isValid = USERNAME_REGEX.test(value);
```

but this will be enough 
```ts
if (!isUserNameValid(value)) {
 return;
}
```
--- 
As for the question about logging in locally, I will not be able to help you, I do not have the knowledge on this subject yet, I think @dansholds will be able to help you.
ok, i'll look into implementing it. thanks for the help
